reasoning: The pain is plausible and paid alternatives exist, but the audience is too broad and real seller demand has not been validated.
known: OpenAI has current image generation and image editing APIs suitable for a technical prototype.
Amazon product image requirements create a concrete compliance surface for sellers.
Existing ecommerce image editing services show sellers already pay for product-image cleanup.
assumed: Small cross-border sellers feel enough pain to pay for platform-specific image packs.
Sellers prefer a done-for-you workflow over a prompt-heavy generic AI tool.
The first wedge should be Amazon/cross-border sellers, not all Taobao, JD, and cross-border platforms at once.
toValidate: Does AWS mean Amazon marketplace or Amazon Web Services?
Find five real sellers who recently edited or outsourced product images.
Verify current Taobao and JD official image rules from seller-console or platform docs.
Test whether sellers pay for compliance assurance, conversion lift, speed, or style quality.
concerns: Generic AI product-image tools already exist; the wedge must be marketplace-specific compliance and workflow.
Generated images can create trust, refund, or policy risk if they misrepresent the product.
China marketplace rules and cross-border platform rules differ enough that a broad first product will be brittle.
nextSteps: Narrow the first validation segment to cross-border Amazon sellers with 10-200 active SKUs.
Run 10 seller interviews and ask for their last rejected or underperforming product image.
Offer a manual paid SKU image pack before building SaaS automation.
Collect before/after image sets and platform rejection reasons as the first evidence corpus.

snapshot.targetUser: Cross-border Amazon sellers first; Taobao/JD later.
snapshot.painfulSituation: Product image sets must be attractive, compliant, and platform-specific.
snapshot.currentWorkaround: Manual editing, outsourced image services, and generic AI tools.
snapshot.desiredOutcome: Marketplace-ready image pack from one raw product photo.
snapshot.firstReachableChannel: Amazon seller communities and cross-border ecommerce operator groups.

demandRealAndFrequent.notes: Marketplace image rules and paid alternatives support plausibility; real seller interviews are still missing.

willingnessToPay.firstPayingCustomerPlan: Sell 5 manual SKU packs to cross-border sellers before building a self-serve app.

landingPageTest.headline: Turn one raw product photo into a marketplace-ready Amazon image pack.
landingPageTest.successThreshold: 5 qualified seller calls or 2 paid SKU packs within 7 days

handoffSummary.summary: Do not build broad automation yet; validate a paid, manual SKU image-pack service for Amazon/cross-border sellers first.
